steps.text: Subtract the AL (here equal to zero) from the survey unit measurements.
Perform a one-sided WRS test on the adjusted measurements.
Perform the Quantile test on the adjusted survey unit measurements.
Judge the survey unit as not exceeding the release criteria.
Calculate the standard deviation of the net count rate (statistical counting uncertainty) using Equation 6-17.
Calculate the standard deviation associated with the total uncertainty using Equation 6-18.
Remove DRPs (Discrete Radioactive Particles) soon after their discovery during characterization surveys.
Perform site-specific exposure dose/risk calculations using an acceptable exposure pathway model.
Show the calculation of the number of data points needed for statistical testing and the use of Table 5.3 in Example 7.
Correct '86.6 m2' to '99.7 m2' in Example 8 on page 5-41.
Correct the reference in Example 9 on page 5-45 from Table I.11 to Table I.12.
Explain in detail the method used to select the locations depicted on the diagram using Table I.12 in Example 9.
Determine the ratio between the concentration of each radionuclide in the mixture and the concentration for that radionuclide in an appropriate listing of default values.
Sum the ratios for all radionuclides in the mixture.
Comment on the effectiveness of the new organization of Chapter 4 (Considerations for Planning Surveys) to improve the understandability of the Chapter.
Comment on the effectiveness of moving derivations from Chapter 5 to Appendix O to improve the understandability of the Chapter.
Correct Page 5-36, line 3 to read '…treatment of areas of elevated radioactive materials…'.
Resolve notation inconsistency in Section 5.3.5 (Equations 5-1 and 5-2) regarding the definition of 'A'.
Define 'A (grid area)' and 'A (surface area)' in Equations 5-3 and 5-4 and in 'Symbols, Nomenclature, and Notations' on Page xxviii.
Use 'AEA' notation consistently throughout Section 5.3.2 and the entire MARSSIM document.
Remove Equations 5-5 and 5-6 to avoid confusion.
Clarify the grid area calculation statement in Page 5-41, Example 8.
Clarify the Unity Rule derivation with regard to DCGLEMC and DCGLW in Equation 8-4 (Section 8.6.2).
Remove the reference to the quench agent.
Replace 'sensitivity' with 'capability' on Page 6-36, line 26.
Remove 'organic' and refer to them as 'plastic scintillators' on Page 6-36, line 1.
Replace 'sensitivity' with 'capability' on Page 6-42, line 4.

preconditions.text: Applying the WRS test for Scenario B
If the WRS test is not rejected
If the Quantile test also is not rejected
Ensure individual uncertainties are relatively small, symmetric about zero, and independent of one another.
The historical site assessment (HAS) identifies the possibility of DRPs and one is found.
Actual radioactive material characterization data are available.
More than one radionuclide is present at a concentration that is distinguishable from background and where a single concentration comparison does not apply.

constraints.text: Values that exceed an investigational limit are to be examined more closely, despite the outcome of the non-parametric tests.
Do not apply MARSSIM guidance established for area sources or volumetric sources to DRPs, as they are treated as point sources.
Avoid treating the area around a DRP as an EMC (Elevated Measurement Comparison), as it creates an extreme sampling situation due to the microscopic size of DRPs.
The sum of the ratios for all radionuclides in the mixture should not exceed 1.
